title: Natural Gas Tax Repeal Act
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
plain_summary: This bill gets rid of a government program that made oil and gas companies pay fees when they released too much methane gas into the air. The program was run by the Environmental Protection Agency and was meant to help companies reduce pollution. The bill also takes back any leftover money that was saved for this program. Only big companies that released a lot of greenhouse gases had to pay these fees.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Natural Gas Tax Repeal Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Repeal
sections.text: Section one hundred thirty six of the Clean Air Act is completely removed from the law. This section created a program that charged fees for methane gas pollution and provided rewards to petroleum and natural gas companies that reduced waste.

sections.title: Section 3 Rescission
sections.text: Any money that was set aside for the methane program but has not yet been spent is taken back by the government. This applies to all unused money from the program as it existed before this new law was passed.
